raw_response: {"rating": 0.1, "lean": "Center", "explanation": "This is a news report presenting survey findings from the Pew Research Center on American views of abortion. The author's own prose is almost entirely descriptive and data-driven, presenting polling numbers across demographic groups without advocating for any position. The near-neutral rating reflects the largely balanced presentation of statistical findings with minimal authorial framing. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's own prose is confined almost entirely to presenting percentages and demographic breakdowns, with no normative language favoring either pro-choice or pro-life positions; (2) Both Republican and Democratic positions are described in symmetric terms — support levels, trends over time, and intra-party divisions are reported for both sides without differential framing; (3) No loaded descriptors are used for either side of the abortion debate; terms like 'legal' and 'illegal' are applied consistently and neutrally throughout; (4) The piece does not foreground sympathetic anecdotes, advocacy sources, or policy recommendations — it is structured purely around survey methodology and findings; (5) A very slight upward lean is noted because the lede foregrounds the 61% majority supporting legal abortion before presenting any counter-data, and the partisan trend narrative emphasizes Democratic growth in support without equivalent framing of Republican stability as a meaningful data point — but this is minimal and does not rise above Center."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report presenting survey findings from the Pew Research Center on American views of abortion. The author's own prose is almost entirely descriptive and data-driven, presenting polling numbers across demographic groups without advocating for any position. The near-neutral rating reflects the largely balanced presentation of statistical findings with minimal authorial framing. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's own prose is confined almost entirely to presenting percentages and demographic breakdowns, with no normative language favoring either pro-choice or pro-life positions; (2) Both Republican and Democratic positions are described in symmetric terms — support levels, trends over time, and intra-party divisions are reported for both sides without differential framing; (3) No loaded descriptors are used for either side of the abortion debate; terms like 'legal' and 'illegal' are applied consistently and neutrally throughout; (4) The piece does not foreground sympathetic anecdotes, advocacy sources, or policy recommendations — it is structured purely around survey methodology and findings; (5) A very slight upward lean is noted because the lede foregrounds the 61% majority supporting legal abortion before presenting any counter-data, and the partisan trend narrative emphasizes Democratic growth in support without equivalent framing of Republican stability as a meaningful data point — but this is minimal and does not rise above Center.

explanation: This is a news report presenting survey findings from the Pew Research Center on American views of abortion. The author's own prose is almost entirely descriptive and data-driven, presenting polling numbers across demographic groups without advocating for any position. The near-neutral rating reflects the largely balanced presentation of statistical findings with minimal authorial framing. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author's own prose is confined almost entirely to presenting percentages and demographic breakdowns, with no normative language favoring either pro-choice or pro-life positions; (2) Both Republican and Democratic positions are described in symmetric terms — support levels, trends over time, and intra-party divisions are reported for both sides without differential framing; (3) No loaded descriptors are used for either side of the abortion debate; terms like 'legal' and 'illegal' are applied consistently and neutrally throughout; (4) The piece does not foreground sympathetic anecdotes, advocacy sources, or policy recommendations — it is structured purely around survey methodology and findings; (5) A very slight upward lean is noted because the lede foregrounds the 61% majority supporting legal abortion before presenting any counter-data, and the partisan trend narrative emphasizes Democratic growth in support without equivalent framing of Republican stability as a meaningful data point — but this is minimal and does not rise above Center.
